purpose: Keep SymLiquid and Theseus Hive training/inference on the fastest safe local backend, with CPU used only as orchestration or fallback.

backend_priority.weak_client: run tiny local arms and proxy larger checkpoint/live-model work to the best authorized Hive peer

optimization_rules: reuse cached CUDA context, PTX modules, kernels, and streams
keep feature/weight/state buffers resident across repeated launches when the runner supports it
batch enough samples per launch to amortize H2D, kernel, and D2H costs
treat CUDA fallback as a blocker for training profiles
record kernel launches, H2D, kernel, D2H, examples/sec, GPU name, VRAM, and utilization for every run
cache hashed BabyLM pair features before converting to MLX tensors
keep arrays float32 and avoid Python list-to-MLX conversion when numpy is available
report cache hit rate, feature build time, MLX transfer time, train time, and eval time
use MLX for Mac training/inference chunks and keep external inference forbidden

hive.single_machine_policy: do not run concurrent heavy chunks unless resource governor says the profile is safe

autonomy.teacher_escalation: only after local performance evidence shows an architecture wall
autonomy.speed_principle: Faster training/inference is effective extra lifetime for the local model, but never bypass safety, license, or leakage gates.
